ab: pakbalayq mi su’ i paqucan quw?
ch: A:我可以訪問你嗎?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: balayq ga!
ch: B:可以呀!

ab: pakbalayq mi su’ i paqucan quw?
ch: A:我可以訪問你嗎?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: laxi, ini’ kapayux ku baqun mu.
ch: B:不要，我所知道的不多。
en: B:Don't, I don't know much about it.

ab: kiya hreq na waw si qaliyan quw? pakbalayq i wahan mi su’ maqut quw?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: uwal, nagaaw mi su’ imuwag.
ch: B:好，我會在家等你。
en: B: Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

ab: kiya a hreq na waw si casan quw? papaqut ci isu’ cu wawwaw na nanabakis na Itaal cuwnga’.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於泰雅族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I would like to ask you about the legendary stories of the Atayal people.

ab: mausa’ ci ragiyax casan, pakaukas ci imuwag yo!
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

ab: isu’ ku paqucay ta’ mha cu gi’, pungan ku kayi ga, isu’ cubalay ku baaq cu wawwaw ru’ Gagagaga’ na nanabakis na Itaal cuwnga’.
ch: A:我聽說您對泰雅族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very knowledgeable about the traditional customs of the Atayal and would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: ini’ kapayux a baqun mu, asikinaga kiya ku baqun mu ga baliy ci palqing cumiyuk.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don't know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: pakbalayq i alan mu cu qalimu’ quw? nakiy ani kanuwan ga parhurhuw ca linanglung ku waw kahani.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: uwal, yani cubalay!
ch: B:好的，沒問題。
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: paya’ a kaal si la kahani?
ch: A:請問您今年幾歲了?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: maqisu’ kinamlapgan cu mamaspat kaal mu kahani la.
ch: B:我已經98歲了。
en: B: I'm 98 years old.

ab: masilulux su’ cu lawkah su’ wala! ini’ patuqaqalinga’ a linanglung su’.
ch: A:98歲？您既長壽又健康呀!思維還這麼清楚。
en: A:98 also? You live a long and healthy life! You're still thinking clearly.

ab: aqeh la! nabakis ci la cubalay.
ch: B:沒有啦!我已經老了。
en: B: No, I'm not! I'm old.

ab: minukahul si inu’?
ch: A:請問您來自哪裡?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: minukahul ci Tabilas.
ch: B:我來自圓墩。
en: B: I'm from Roundabout.

ab: Itaal ku kanayril su’ uwiy quw?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是泰雅族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also an Atayal?

ab: iqaat, Siputu’ na ihiya’.
ch: B:不，她是客家人。
en: B:No, she's a guest.

ab: baaq si kumaal cu kayi’ na nunuwan?
ch: A:您會說哪些語言?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: baaq ci kumaal cu kayi’ na Matu’uwal, Tu’ya’, Siputu’ ru’ baaq cu hayhayi’ cu kayi’ na Kamciyulang uwiy.
ch: B: 我會說汶水泰雅語國語客語還略通閩南語。
en: B: I speak Brunei Atayal, Mandarin, Hakka and Minnan.

ab: paqasun mu cubalay ku pinaskakaru’ mi ki isu’ sawni’, kalani tikay ku pisaneq na qaqaluwaap quw?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It's a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: uwal, mamati’ ci musa’ qumaluwaap i ragiyax cu mugumalak cu na’.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: mahuway su’! balayq cubalay ku pinasibaq su’ kuwing sawni’.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: cubalay ku pinqaqwu’ mu sawni ka sinbil na nanabakis.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: pasikiaw ti runi ku pinskakaru’ ta’, mahuway su’ cubalay ka sipasbabaq cami kahabaag.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That's all for today's interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: kaa ubaga’! maskakaruay ta’ babaw niya’ lawwiy.
ch: B:不客氣，我們再聊。
en: B: You're welcome, let's talk again.

ab: mausa’ ci la, nagaaw mi muwng babaw niya’ lawwiy ku wawwaw na nanabakis cuwunga’ ka payux.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I'm going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: uwal la! nahahiyaw i musa’ kiy! mapapugagagayay ta’ la!
ch: B:好喔!請慢走。再見。
en: B: Okay! Take care. See you later.
